17	// ADR-022 §Worker-server interface (rev 5). Workers are autonomous; the
18	// server's view of who's working is derived from best-effort event reports.
19	// This file implements the three ingestion endpoints:
20	//
21	//   POST /api/workers/register        — issue a worker_id for an identity envelope
22	//   POST /api/workers/{id}/event      — append a worker-emitted event
23	//   POST /api/workers/{id}/backfill   — replay buffered events after reconnect
24	//
25	// In-memory derived view backed by an append-only JSONL log at
26	// .ddx/server/worker-events.jsonl under the server's WorkingDir. Both the
27	// registry and the log are best-effort: if the server crashes the workers
28	// keep operating and re-register on next probe.
29	
30	const workerIngestMaxBodyBytes = 1 << 20 // 1 MiB cap on register/event/backfill payloads.
31	
32	// errUnknownWorker signals that a worker_id is not present in the in-memory
33	// registry. Surfaced as 410 Gone so the worker re-registers within the same
34	// probe cycle (ADR-022 §Probe + freshness state model).
35	var errUnknownWorker = errors.New("unknown_worker")
36	
37	// workerIdentity is the thin envelope POSTed on /api/workers/register.
38	type workerIdentity struct {
39		ProjectRoot  string    `json:"project_root"`
40		Harness      string    `json:"harness"`
41		Model        string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
42		ExecutorPID  int       `json:"executor_pid"`
43		ExecutorHost string    `json:"executor_host"`
44		StartedAt    time.Time `json:"started_at"`
45	}
46	
47	// workerEvent mirrors a single bead-event the worker would write to its
48	// local event log. The wire payload is intentionally minimal — the bead's
49	// local log remains the authoritative copy.
50	type workerEvent struct {
51		BeadID    string          `json:"bead_id"`
52		AttemptID string          `json:"attempt_id"`
53		Kind      string          `json:"kind"`
54		Body      json.RawMessage `json:"body,omitempty"`
55	}
56	
57	// workerBackfillRequest carries the worker's NotConnected ring buffer.
58	// dropped=true means oldest events were silently dropped at the worker
59	// (rev 5: HadDroppedBackfill flag surfaces "consult bead-local logs").
60	type workerBackfillRequest struct {
61		Events  []workerEvent `json:"events"`
62		Dropped bool          `json:"dropped,omitempty"`
63	}
64	
65	// workerRecord is the per-worker derived state the server holds in memory.
66	type workerRecord struct {
67		WorkerID            string         `json:"worker_id"`
68		Identity            workerIdentity `json:"identity"`
69		RegisteredAt        time.Time      `json:"registered_at"`
70		LastEventAt         time.Time      `json:"last_event_at"`
71		MirrorFailuresCount int            `json:"mirror_failures_count"`
72		HadDroppedBackfill  bool           `json:"had_dropped_backfill"`
73		CurrentBead         string         `json:"current_bead,omitempty"`
74		CurrentAttempt      string         `json:"current_attempt,omitempty"`
75	}
76	
77	// workerIngestRegistry holds the in-memory derived view + owns the
78	// append-only event log. All exported methods are safe for concurrent use.
79	type workerIngestRegistry struct {
80		mu       sync.Mutex
81		workers  map[string]*workerRecord
82		logPath  string
83		logFile  *os.File
84		openOnce sync.Once
85		openErr  error
86	}
87	
88	func newWorkerIngestRegistry(workingDir string) *workerIngestRegistry {
89		return &workerIngestRegistry{
90			workers: make(map[string]*workerRecord),
91			logPath: filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "server", "worker-events.jsonl"),
92		}
93	}
94	
95	// register stores a new worker record and returns the issued worker_id.
96	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) register(id workerIdentity) *workerRecord {
97		now := time.Now().UTC()
98		rec := &workerRecord{
99			WorkerID:     newWorkerID(),
100			Identity:     id,
101			RegisteredAt: now,
102			LastEventAt:  now,
103		}
104		r.mu.Lock()
105		r.workers[rec.WorkerID] = rec
106		r.mu.Unlock()
107		return rec
108	}
109	
110	// recordEvent appends ev to the JSONL log and refreshes the worker's
111	// last_event_at timestamp. Returns errUnknownWorker if workerID is not
112	// registered.
113	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) recordEvent(workerID string, ev workerEvent) error {
114		r.mu.Lock()
115		rec, ok := r.workers[workerID]
116		if !ok {
117			r.mu.Unlock()
118			return errUnknownWorker
119		}
120		rec.LastEventAt = time.Now().UTC()
121		if ev.BeadID != "" {
122			rec.CurrentBead = ev.BeadID
123		}
124		if ev.AttemptID != "" {
125			rec.CurrentAttempt = ev.AttemptID
126		}
127		r.mu.Unlock()
128		return r.append(workerID, ev)
129	}
130	
131	// recordBackfill appends every event in the buffer; flips
132	// HadDroppedBackfill when the worker reports dropped overflow.
133	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) recordBackfill(workerID string, req workerBackfillRequest) error {
134		r.mu.Lock()
135		rec, ok := r.workers[workerID]
136		if !ok {
137			r.mu.Unlock()
138			return errUnknownWorker
139		}
140		if req.Dropped {
141			rec.HadDroppedBackfill = true
142		}
143		if len(req.Events) > 0 {
144			rec.LastEventAt = time.Now().UTC()
145			last := req.Events[len(req.Events)-1]
146			if last.BeadID != "" {
147				rec.CurrentBead = last.BeadID
148			}
149			if last.AttemptID != "" {
150				rec.CurrentAttempt = last.AttemptID
151			}
152		}
153		r.mu.Unlock()
154		for _, ev := range req.Events {
155			if err := r.append(workerID, ev); err != nil {
156				return err
157			}
158		}
159		return nil
160	}
161	
162	// snapshot returns a copy of the registry for read-only inspection (tests
163	// and future GraphQL consumers). The returned slice is a stable copy; the
164	// records themselves are pointers into the registry and must be treated
165	// as read-only.
166	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) snapshot() []*workerRecord {
167		r.mu.Lock()
168		defer r.mu.Unlock()
169		out := make([]*workerRecord, 0, len(r.workers))
170		for _, rec := range r.workers {
171			copy := *rec
172			out = append(out, &copy)
173		}
174		return out
175	}

177	// loggedEvent is the on-disk shape of a single line in worker-events.jsonl.
178	// Wraps the worker-supplied event with the receiving worker_id and the
179	// server's timestamp.
180	type loggedEvent struct {
181		WorkerID  string          `json:"worker_id"`
182		Timestamp time.Time       `json:"timestamp"`
183		BeadID    string          `json:"bead_id,omitempty"`
184		AttemptID string          `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
185		Kind      string          `json:"kind"`
186		Body      json.RawMessage `json:"body,omitempty"`
187	}
188	
189	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) append(workerID string, ev workerEvent) error {
190		r.mu.Lock()
191		defer r.mu.Unlock()
192		r.openOnce.Do(func() {
193			if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(r.logPath), 0o755); err != nil {
194				r.openErr = err
195				return
196			}
197			f, err := os.OpenFile(r.logPath, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
198			if err != nil {
199				r.openErr = err
200				return
201			}
202			r.logFile = f
203		})
204		if r.openErr != nil {
205			return r.openErr
206		}
207		line, err := json.Marshal(loggedEvent{
208			WorkerID:  workerID,
209			Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
210			BeadID:    ev.BeadID,
211			AttemptID: ev.AttemptID,
212			Kind:      ev.Kind,
213			Body:      ev.Body,
214		})
215		if err != nil {
216			return err
217		}
218		line = append(line, '\n')
219		_, err = r.logFile.Write(line)
220		return err
221	}
222	
223	// close releases the log file handle. Safe to call when the file was
224	// never opened. Used on server shutdown / test cleanup.
225	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) close() error {
226		r.mu.Lock()
227		defer r.mu.Unlock()
228		if r.logFile == nil {
229			return nil
230		}
231		err := r.logFile.Close()
232		r.logFile = nil
233		return err
234	}

236	func newWorkerID() string {
237		var b [12]byte
238		if _, err := rand.Read(b[:]); err != nil {
239			// crypto/rand failure is unrecoverable; fall back to a timestamp
240			// stub so the path is non-empty (registration still succeeds and
241			// the operator sees a malformed-looking ID in the UI).
242			return fmt.Sprintf("wkr-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
243		}
244		return "wkr-" + hex.EncodeToString(b[:])
245	}

249	func (s *Server) handleWorkerRegister(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
250		var id workerIdentity
251		if err := decodeWorkerIngestBody(r, &id); err != nil {
252			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
253			return
254		}
255		if id.ProjectRoot == "" {
256			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "project_root required"})
257			return
258		}
259		rec := s.workerIngest.register(id)
260		writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{"worker_id": rec.WorkerID})
261	}
262	
263	func (s *Server) handleWorkerEvent(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
264		workerID := r.PathValue("id")
265		if workerID == "" {
266			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "worker id required"})
267			return
268		}
269		var ev workerEvent
270		if err := decodeWorkerIngestBody(r, &ev); err != nil {
271			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
272			return
273		}
274		if ev.Kind == "" {
275			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "kind required"})
276			return
277		}
278		if err := s.workerIngest.recordEvent(workerID, ev); err != nil {
279			if errors.Is(err, errUnknownWorker) {
280				writeJSON(w, http.StatusGone, map[string]string{"error": "unknown_worker"})
281				return
282			}
283			writeJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
284			return
285		}
286		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
287	}

289	func (s *Server) handleWorkerBackfill(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
290		workerID := r.PathValue("id")
291		if workerID == "" {
292			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "worker id required"})
293			return
294		}
295		var req workerBackfillRequest
296		if err := decodeWorkerIngestBody(r, &req); err != nil {
297			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
298			return
299		}
300		if err := s.workerIngest.recordBackfill(workerID, req); err != nil {
301			if errors.Is(err, errUnknownWorker) {
302				writeJSON(w, http.StatusGone, map[string]string{"error": "unknown_worker"})
303				return
304			}
305			writeJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
306			return
307		}
308		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
309	}

311	// workerIngestView is the wire shape returned by GET /api/workers — a
312	// flattened snapshot of the runtime registry used by `ddx agent doctor`
313	// (ADR-022 step 6). The on-disk .ddx/workers/<id>/status.json layout
314	// remains the fallback source for one alpha release.
315	type workerIngestView struct {
316		WorkerID            string    `json:"worker_id"`
317		ProjectRoot         string    `json:"project_root"`
318		Harness             string    `json:"harness,omitempty"`
319		Model               string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
320		RegisteredAt        time.Time `json:"registered_at"`
321		LastEventAt         time.Time `json:"last_event_at"`
322		MirrorFailuresCount int       `json:"mirror_failures_count"`
323		HadDroppedBackfill  bool      `json:"had_dropped_backfill,omitempty"`
324		Freshness           string    `json:"freshness"`
325	}
326	
327	// handleWorkerIngestList serves GET /api/workers: a snapshot of the
328	// in-memory runtime registry (ADR-022 rev 5). Empty list when no workers
329	// have registered. Fields mirror workerRecord plus a derived freshness
330	// classification.
331	func (s *Server) handleWorkerIngestList(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
332		snap := s.workerIngest.snapshot()
333		now := time.Now().UTC()
334		out := make([]workerIngestView, 0, len(snap))
335		for _, rec := range snap {
336			out = append(out, workerIngestView{
337				WorkerID:            rec.WorkerID,
338				ProjectRoot:         rec.Identity.ProjectRoot,
339				Harness:             rec.Identity.Harness,
340				Model:               rec.Identity.Model,
341				RegisteredAt:        rec.RegisteredAt,
342				LastEventAt:         rec.LastEventAt,
343				MirrorFailuresCount: rec.MirrorFailuresCount,
344				HadDroppedBackfill:  rec.HadDroppedBackfill,
345				Freshness:           freshnessState(rec, now),
346			})
347		}
348		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i].RegisteredAt.After(out[j].RegisteredAt) })
349		writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, out)
350	}
351	
352	func decodeWorkerIngestBody(r *http.Request, v any) error {
353		r.Body = http.MaxBytesReader(nil, r.Body, workerIngestMaxBodyBytes)
354		if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(v); err != nil {
355			return fmt.Errorf("invalid request body: %w", err)
356		}
357		return nil
358	}
359	
360	// freshnessState classifies a worker by recency of its last_event_at against
361	// the probe interval (ADR-022 rev 5: connected ≤2× probe; stale ≤10× probe;
362	// disconnected otherwise). Probe default is 30s, hard-coded here because the
363	// per-worker poll_interval is not part of the rev-5 register payload.
364	func freshnessState(rec *workerRecord, now time.Time) string {
365		const probeInterval = 30 * time.Second
366		age := now.Sub(rec.LastEventAt)
367		switch {
368		case age <= 2*probeInterval:
369			return "connected"
370		case age <= 10*probeInterval:
371			return "stale"
372		default:
373			return "disconnected"
374		}
375	}

277	// Tailscale ts-net listener is started alongside the standard localhost listener.
278	func (s *Server) ListenAndServe() error {
279		release, err := acquireSingletonLock()
280		if err != nil {

282		}
283		defer release()
284		s.installSingletonReleaseOnSignal(release)
285		s.writeAddrFile("http")

292			}()

294			// ts-net listener
295			go func() {
296				errCh <- s.listenTsnet()
297			}()

299			return <-errCh
300		}

378	// addr file. Returns "" if none is present, or if the recorded pid is not
379	// alive (in which case a warning is emitted and the stale file is best-effort
380	// cleared so callers fall back to the default URL).
381	func ReadServerAddr() string {
382		type addrFile struct {
383			URL string `json:"url"`
384			PID int    `json:"pid"`
385		}
386		path := filepath.Join(serverAddrDir(), "server.addr")
387		data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
388		if err != nil {
389			return ""
390		}
391		var af addrFile
392		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &af); err != nil {
393			return ""
394		}
395		if af.PID > 0 && !processAlive(af.PID) {
396			fmt.Fprintf(addrFallbackWarnWriter,
397				"warning: server.addr points to a dead pid %d; falling back to default 127.0.0.1:7743\n",
398				af.PID)
399			_ = os.Remove(path)
400			return ""
401		}
402		return af.URL

405	// installSingletonReleaseOnSignal arranges for the singleton lock to be

409	// run, but the lock release fires explicitly here.
410	func (s *Server) installSingletonReleaseOnSignal(release func()) {
411		ch := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
412		signal.Notify(ch, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)

414			<-ch
415			release()
